text: The probability ordering is real but the edge is thin (overall Brier near the base rate). '52w-high breakout' calibrates best (Brier 0.2348), while 'RSI<30 oversold' is the least reliable (Brier 0.2519) — its raw probabilities are the most over/under-confident. Walk-forward calibration improves out-of-sample. Suggestion: down-weight 'RSI<30 oversold' or apply isotonic recalibration to it before combining signals.
